competitor_patterns: Bikerumor dominates with extreme depth (24,353 words) combining tested reviews, buyer guides, comparison tables, and FAQs in a single authoritative hub page — covering multiple product angles in one URL rather than splitting topics
Top-ranking pages use structured comparison tables (Bikerumor, 220Triathlon) with specs side-by-side, enabling quick scanning and satisfying both users and Google's feature-snippet triggers

All top 3 competitors include explicit 'how we test' or 'why trust us' credibility sections, establishing editorial authority that pure brand blogs lack — this directly reduces bounce and signals E-E-A-T

Competitors refresh content frequently (Bikerumor updated May 21, 2026; 220Triathlon May 7, 2026) with explicit 'updated' timestamps in snippets, which improves CTR in SERPs by signalling freshness to searchers

High-ranking pages use H2s as product/brand names (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) turning each section into a rankable entity — capturing comparison and alternative searches beyond the main keyword
our_gaps: All our posts are 1,153–1,712 words — 10–20x shorter than Bikerumor's 24,353-word hub; we lack the content depth to compete on broad 'best cycling glasses' terms and cannot capture long-tail variation within a single page

None of our recent posts include a comparison table — the single highest-impact structural element for ranking on buyer-intent queries and triggering Google rich results; competitors use tables on 3 of 4 analysed pages

Our H2 structure is problem/solution/brand focused (always leads to Velluto) rather than using competitor brand names as H2s — we miss all 'Oakley vs', 'Tifosi alternative', 'POC Devour vs' comparison searches

No 'how we tested' or independent credibility section exists in any recent post — our content reads as brand-owned promotion, reducing E-E-A-T signals and making AI overviews unlikely to cite us as a neutral source

We have no long-form hub/pillar page targeting the primary 'best cycling glasses 2026' keyword cluster — we publish multiple thin posts on sub-topics but lack a single authoritative resource that can accumulate backlinks and ranking signals
keyword_opportunities: best cycling sunglasses under €100 2026 — 220Triathlon ranks with budget-angle content; Velluto's price positioning makes this a direct commercial fit with no current coverage

interchangeable lens cycling glasses comparison — velo.outsideonline.com ranks #1 with a multi-brand comparison review; we published on this topic but without naming/comparing specific competitors like Smith Pivlock, Oakley Jawbreaker, or Tifosi
lightweight cycling glasses under 30g — no competitor page specifically targets weight as primary filter; Velluto's 25g frame is a factual differentiator that maps to a real purchase decision query

cycling glasses anti-fog road cycling — anti-fog is a high-frequency complaint in cycling forums and buyer reviews; none of the top-ranking pages have a dedicated anti-fog section or target this as a primary keyword

cycling glasses 30 day trial risk free — zero competitors offer this; it is an untapped branded + category keyword that captures high-intent buyers at the decision stage and has no current SERP competition
next_post_guidelines: Write one long-form hub post targeting 'best cycling glasses 2026' at minimum 4,000 words — include a comparison table of 6–8 glasses (including named competitors like Oakley Sutro Lite, Tifosi Rail, POC Devour) with Velluto StradaPro as the recommended value pick; this is the single highest-leverage content gap

Include a named-competitor H2 section for each major brand (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite: Who It's For', 'Tifosi Rail: Pros and Cons') — this captures comparison searches and signals topical depth without being promotional

Add a 'How We Evaluate Cycling Glasses' section (100–150 words) to every future post covering criteria: weight, lens system, UV rating, fit adjustability, anti-fog, price — this establishes E-E-A-T and mirrors what top competitors do to build trust

Every post must include at least one HTML comparison table — minimum columns: Weight, Lens Type, Anti-Fog, UV Rating, Price, Trial Policy; tables trigger rich results and dramatically improve dwell time on buyer-intent queries

Target the 'lightweight cycling glasses' angle explicitly: open with Velluto's 25g claim, provide a weight comparison table against Oakley Sutro Lite (~36g), POC Devour (~30g), and Tifosi Rail (~28g) — weight is a factual, verifiable differentiator that competitors do not lead with

Add an explicit publication and last-updated date in the first 100 words of every post (e.g. 'Last updated: June 2026') — this improves CTR by replicating the freshness signal that Bikerumor and 220Triathlon show in snippets

End every post with a structured FAQ block of minimum 5 questions using actual search phrasing (e.g. 'Are 25g cycling glasses worth it?', 'Do interchangeable lenses fog up?', 'What is the best anti-fog cycling glass in 2026?') — target featured snippet positions with concise 40–60 word answers
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every future post immediately — even a simple 4-column, 5-row table comparing Velluto StradaPro against 3–4 competitors on weight, lens system, UV400, anti-fog, and price satisfies both user intent and Google's structured data appetite; this is the highest-ROI structural change given that 0 of our recent 4 posts include one

Rewrite all meta descriptions to include a specific numeric hook and a direct CTR trigger — format: '[Number] tested, [key differentiator], [action]' e.g. 'Tested: 6 cycling glasses ranked by weight, anti-fog & value. See why 25g StradaPro beats Oakley at half the price.' — current posts lack specificity that drives clicks

Implement internal linking from every new post back to a single 'best cycling glasses 2026' hub page (to be created) using exact-match and partial-match anchor text — this concentrates link equity on the highest-value target page instead of fragmenting authority across 8+ thin posts
meta_title_fixes: No GSC data available yet — meta title fixes cannot be prioritised by CTR or impression volume. content_quick_wins: No GSC data available yet — near-top-3 keyword opportunities cannot be identified from position data. Placeholder: once GSC is live, filter for keywords in positions 4–15 with >50 impressions; for each, audit whether the ranking page has a comparison table and FAQ — if not, add both as the primary push tactic, as these are the two structural elements most correlated with top-3 ranking in the competitor set analysed.
geo_angles: Structure a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite' comparison page with a clear verdict section using natural language ('The StradaPro is 11g lighter than the Sutro Lite and costs 40% less — making it the rational choice for weight-conscious road cyclists') — AI overviews and Perplexity pull from verdict-style, factual comparison content with named entities and specific numbers

Create a 'What are the lightest cycling glasses in 2026?' answer page targeting the AI Overview format: open with a direct one-sentence answer ('The lightest widely available cycling glasses in 2026 weigh between 23g and 28g; the Velluto StradaPro at 25g sits at the top of this range'), follow with a weight comparison table, and cite UV400 and anti-fog specs — Perplexity and ChatGPT prioritise pages that answer a specific question in the first 100 words with verifiable data

Publish a 'cycling glasses buying guide 2026' page structured as a decision tree (question → recommendation) covering face shape, lens type, budget, and ride type — AI systems frequently cite comprehensive buying guides as authoritative sources because they satisfy multiple sub-queries in one resource; include Velluto's 30-day trial as the risk-mitigation answer to the 'what if it doesn't fit?' decision node
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'We Read Bikerumor's 24,000-Word Cycling Glasses Guide So You Don't Have To' | Key differentiator: synthesise their top picks into a decision framework, then show how Velluto StradaPro matches the winner's specs at a lower price point | Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g weight + interchangeable lenses + 30-day trial vs the premium picks they recommend at 2–3x the price

COMPETITOR: velo.outsideonline.com (ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses') — angle: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: Smith Pivlock vs Oakley vs Velluto StradaPro — Tested on Real Rides' | Key differentiator: their review covers Smith and premium brands only; Velluto offers the same click-in, tool-free system at a fraction of the price | Velluto advantage to lead with: VellutoPuro clear + VellutoVisione high-contrast lenses included, 30-day risk-free trial, vs premium brands requiring separate lens purchases at €50–€80 each

COMPETITOR: 220Triathlon (ranks for budget cycling glasses) — angle: 'Best Cycling Glasses Under €100 in 2026: Budget Picks vs Velluto StradaPro — Is the Price Gap Worth It?' | Key differentiator: 220Triathlon recommends cheap glasses with no lens system and no trial; Velluto offers near-premium specs (25g, UV400, anti-fog, interchangeable lenses) at a mid-range price with zero purchase risk | Velluto advantage to lead with: 30-day risk-free trial eliminates the main objection to spending more than budget options — if it's not right, return it
